name: My Bouillabaisse
recipeInstructions:
1. Skin the fish and cut into serving-sized pieces. Use fillets, including flounder or other firm white fish as a substitute.
2. Reserve the broth from the steamed clams for use in the bouillabaise.
3. Heat olive oil in a large pot and cook garlic and onion until soft over medium heat.
4. Add the leek and cook for 10 minutes.
5. Add tomatoes and sugar; simmer for 5 minutes.
6. Add all remaining ingredients except seafood, then simmer for 30 minutes, covered.
7. Strain the broth (discarding orange zest and bay leaf), puree the vegetables with some of the broth, and return to the pot. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
8. This portion can be refrigerated or frozen until ready to serve.
9. Strain the broth from the steamed clams and add to the pot.
10. Add the clams (diced if preferred) along with the remaining seafood and the dark leek portion; simmer for 3-5 minutes, adjusting seasonings as needed.
11. Ladle into bowls and serve with garlic bread and Sauce Rouille.
